name: Roux
description: A block-building method that relies heavily on M-slice moves. Known for low move count and ergonomic turning. Growing in popularity among top solvers.

steps.name: First Block (FB)
steps.description: Build a 1x2x3 block on the left side of the cube, containing the DL edge, two F2L pairs.
tips: Practice multiple solutions for same scramble

steps.name: Second Block (SB)
steps.description: Build a 1x2x3 block on the right side of the cube, mirroring the first block.
tips: Look ahead to DR edge during FB

steps.name: CMLL
steps.description: Solve the corners of the last layer while ignoring M-slice edges. 42 algorithms.
tips: CMLL has easier recognition than COLL

steps.name: LSE (Last Six Edges)
steps.description: Solve the remaining 6 edges using only M and U moves. Three sub-steps: EO, UL/UR, EP.

creator: Gilles Roux
